events.title: Road to 250 Season Opening
events.description: The gates open on Tennessee's most historic year. Step through time to 1791, where government was born and a nation took shape. Be among the first 250 Tennesseans enrolled to have your name read aloud on July 4th—America's 250th birthday.

events.title: Spring Break Camp Week 1
events.description: Trade screens for sawdust and adventure. Campers dip candles, fire the forge, feed heritage animals, and learn skills frontier children mastered by age ten. Johnson City & Bristol schools week. Lunch provided.

events.title: Spring Break Camp Week 2
events.description: Trade screens for sawdust and adventure. Campers dip candles, fire the forge, feed heritage animals, and learn skills frontier children mastered by age ten. Sullivan County schools week. Lunch provided.

events.title: James P. Byrd Guest Lecture: Colonial Religion
events.speaker: Dr. James P. Byrd
events.description: What did frontier settlers believe—and why did it matter? Dr. Byrd, author of 'Sacred Scripture, Sacred War,' reveals how Bible, pulpit, and prayer shaped the men who marched to Kings Mountain.

events.title: Lisa Bennett as Mary Patton: Powder for Kings Mountain
events.speaker: Lisa Bennett
events.description: 500 pounds of gunpowder. One determined woman. Without Mary Patton's powder, there would be no victory at Kings Mountain. Through first-person interpretation, Lisa Bennett brings this overlooked heroine face-to-face with you.

events.title: Sullivan County 250
events.description: Sullivan County men left these fields for Kings Mountain. Their wives and children stayed behind, undefended. Hear the stories the textbooks left out—the gambles, the sacrifices, the families who risked everything.

events.title: Woolly Days & Colonial Gardening
events.description: Hear the shears clip through fleece. Watch raw wool become thread on a spinning wheel. Dig your hands into soil where heirloom seeds take root. Two days of fiber arts, heritage gardening, and the rhythms of spring on a working 1791 homestead.

events.title: Homeschool Day - Spring
events.description: History jumps off the page when you can touch it. Your students will dip quill pens, card wool, and hear stories from interpreters who've walked in William Cobb's shoes. TN Standards-aligned activities and take-home materials included.

events.title: All In: The Overmountain Men's Gamble
events.speaker: Thomas Bachelor
events.description: They bet everything—their farms, their families, their lives. The 1780 march to Kings Mountain wasn't patriotic fervor. It was a desperate gamble by men who knew they might never come home. Thomas Bachelor strips away the myth.

events.title: Early Frontier Days & Colonial Trade Fair
events.description: Musket smoke drifts past canvas tents. Traders hawk pewter, powder, and handmade goods. Militia camps come alive with drill and fire. Three days of the largest Revolutionary-era gathering in the region, with OVTA. Bigger than ever for America 250.

events.title: William Blount and the Birth of Tennessee Government
events.speaker: Dr. Caroline H. Whitfield
events.description: George Washington sent William Blount west to govern the frontier. Blount chose Rocky Mount—this very ground—as his capitol. Dr. Whitfield reveals the political intrigue, land speculation, and unlikely friendships that birthed Tennessee.

events.title: Tennessee's 230th Birthday
events.description: 230 years ago today, the 16th star joined the flag. Tennessee's journey from territory to state began on this ground. LAST DAY to enroll in First 250—have your name read aloud at Rocky Mount on July 4th.

events.title: The Story of the American Flag
events.speaker: David Doan
events.description: Every star and stripe carries a story. From the Grand Union that flew at Boston to the banner that inspired Key's anthem, David Doan traces the flag's evolution. Followed by the dedication of Rocky Mount's America 250 commemorative flag.

events.title: Stitching Independence
events.description: Two days of stars, stripes, and stitches. Saturday: Tennessee's 230th recognition and our America 250 flag dedication. Sunday: the Betsy Ross Sip & Sew—wine, needles, and the art of flag-making. Limited seats for Sip & Sew.

events.title: Summer Camp Week 1
events.description: Five days as a frontier kid. Fire a flintlock (blanks!). Cook over an open hearth. Chase heritage chickens. Learn the skills that built Tennessee—then take them home. Ages 7-12. Lunch provided.

events.title: Father's Day Weekend at the Forge
events.description: The fire roars. The hammer falls. You and your kid, shoulder to shoulder, forging something real. Create a hand-wrought keepsake together at the anvil—a Father's Day gift money can't buy. 10 pairs only. Reserve now.

events.title: Summer Camp Week 2
events.description: Five days as a frontier kid. Fire a flintlock (blanks!). Cook over an open hearth. Chase heritage chickens. Learn the skills that built Tennessee—then take them home. Ages 7-12. Lunch provided.

events.title: Colonial Independence Day
events.description: America turns 250—and you're standing where Tennessee began. The First 250 ceremony reads each enrolled name aloud. Muskets fire. Period music plays. The flag rises. This is the day. This is the place. Don't watch history—be part of it.

events.title: Back to School at the Frontier
events.description: One last summer hurrah before the books come out. Kids race hoops, toss horseshoes, and play the games frontier children played. Adults sip lemonade in the shade. A family day with no screens, no schedules—just summer fun, 1791 style.

events.title: Homeschool Day - Late Summer
events.description: Launch the school year with history you can hold. Your students card wool, write with quill and ink, and meet William Cobb himself. Perfect kickoff to your homeschool year. TN Standards-aligned activities and take-home materials included.

events.title: Cherokee Heritage Weekend
events.speaker: Dr. Daniel Redbird Wolfe
events.description: The Overmountain settlements sat within Cherokee territory. Treaties were signed, broken, and tested. Hear the other side of the frontier story—told by Cherokee scholars and artisans. Two days of demonstrations, lectures, and perspectives rarely heard.

events.title: First Families of Tennessee Reunion
events.description: Your family built Tennessee. Now come home. The first-ever gathering of descendants from founding families—Cobbs, Shelbys, Seviers. Friday: registered descendants only. Sat-Sun: open to all. Oral histories. Genealogy help. Stories passed down 250 years.

events.title: OVTA Victory Day
events.description: September 1780: Overmountain Men gathered at Sycamore Shoals and marched south to Kings Mountain. September 2026: we retrace their steps. Join OVTA as they commemorate the march that turned the Revolution. Date pending OVTA confirmation.

events.title: Harvest Fest
events.description: The smell of wood smoke and simmering apple butter. Cider pressing. Corn shelling. Sorghum boiling down to molasses. Experience the autumn harvest the way Tennesseans did it for 200 years—with your whole family, working alongside our interpreters.

events.title: Haunting on the Mount
events.description: Candlelight flickers. Shadows move. The frontier had stories they only told after dark—tales of restless spirits, unexplained sounds in the woods, and things that walked the Wilderness Road at night. Six evenings of spooky tours (Oct 15-17 & 22-24). Ages 13+.

events.title: Frontier Christmas
events.description: Cedar garlands. Dried apples. The smell of spiced cider. Before Hallmark, before Santa, there was simply the joy of gathering with family. Experience Christmas the way early Tennesseans celebrated—simple, meaningful, and warm. Wed-Sat through November.

events.title: Candlelight Christmas
events.description: A thousand candles light the grounds. Carolers sing in the cabin. Cider warms cold hands. Rocky Mount's most magical tradition returns—four evenings when the 18th century glows. Friday & Saturday, Dec 4-5 and 11-12. Sells out every year.

blacksmithWorkshops.title: Blacksmith Workshops
blacksmithWorkshops.description: Feel the heat of the forge. Hear the ring of hammer on iron. Shape red-hot metal with your own hands into a hook, nail, or blade—the same way smiths have done for centuries. You leave with something you made. All skill levels welcome.
highlights: Forge your own piece to take home
All materials and safety gear included

hearthCooking.title: Hearth Cooking
hearthCooking.description: No stove. No oven. Just fire, iron, and skill. Learn to bake bread in a Dutch oven, turn a roast on a spit, and simmer stew over coals—then eat what you've cooked. You'll leave knowing how frontier families fed themselves.
highlights: Recipe cards to recreate at home

twilightOnTheMount.title: Twilight on the Mount
twilightOnTheMount.description: Can't make a daytime tour? Join us Thursday evenings. A guided walk through the grounds as the summer heat breaks, with stories of the Cobb family and the government that began here. No crowds, cooler temps, and a different perspective on Tennessee's first capitol.
highlights: Guided walk through the historic grounds
Stories of Rocky Mount's role in Tennessee's founding

homeschoolDays.title: Homeschool Days
homeschoolDays.description: The textbook says 'frontier life was hard.' Here, your students churn butter, card wool, and write with quill pens. They'll remember this long after they've forgotten the chapter. TN Standards-aligned, co-op friendly, take-home materials included.
highlights: Educational materials to continue at home

behindTheScenes.title: Behind the Scenes
behindTheScenes.description: Hold a 230-year-old powder horn in your hands. Walk the collections vault where artifacts not on display await. Feed our heritage animals their morning grain. Coffee and pastries with curators who know every story. 10 guests only. Our most exclusive experience.
